tasks.name: Environment Setup & Dependency Installation
tasks.description: Install EasyEdit (pinned commit), transformers, torch, scipy, scikit-learn, rank_bm25. Download GPT-2-XL weights. Download CounterFact dataset. Verify GPU availability and VRAM. Create output directory structure under exp/.

pilot.pass_criteria: GPT-2-XL loads successfully, EasyEdit ROME imports without error, CounterFact dataset has >= 200 valid entries

tasks.name: Phase 1: ROME Editing Validation
tasks.description: Apply EasyEdit ROME to N CounterFact facts on GPT-2-XL at layer l*=17. Measure editing efficacy (P(new) > P(old)), generalization (paraphrase success), locality (unrelated fact preservation). Save per-fact delta_W tensors to exp/tensors/. Gate: efficacy > 75%.

pilot.pass_criteria: Editing efficacy > 75% on pilot set AND delta_W tensors saved with correct shape (1600, 1600) AND no OOM errors

visualizations.description: ROME editing metrics summary (efficacy, generalization, locality)

tasks.name: Phase 2: TDA Gradient Computation & Validation
tasks.description: For each fact: (1) BM25-retrieve top-20 docs from Wikipedia subset; (2) compute per-document gradients nabla_W L(z_i; theta) at layer l*=17; (3) sanity check: within-cluster vs between-cluster cosine similarity (permutation test, 1000 permutations); (4) aggregate into g_M with BM25 weights. Save g_M tensors to exp/tensors/. Gate: within > between at p < 0.01.

pilot.pass_criteria: Within-cluster cosine > between-cluster cosine at p < 0.05 AND gradient tensors have correct shape (1600, 1600) AND mean gradient norm > 1e-8 (non-degenerate)

visualizations.description: TDA gradient validation: within vs between cluster cosine similarity

tasks.name: Phase 3: Core TECS Measurement with 5 Null Baselines
tasks.description: Compute TECS = cos(vec(delta_W_E), vec(g_M)) at layer l*=17 for all facts. Compute 5 null baselines: Null-A (random-fact swap), Null-B (wrong-layer l*+-5), Null-C (failed-edit subset), Null-D (shuffled gradient), Null-E (random direction). Compute Cohen's d (TECS_real vs each null) with 10,000 bootstrap 95% CI. Bonferroni correction alpha=0.01. Decision gate: d>0.2 for positive path, d<=0.2 for negative path.

pilot.pass_criteria: All 5 null baselines computed without error AND TECS distribution is not degenerate (std > 1e-6) AND bootstrap CI computed successfully

visualizations.description: TECS vs null baselines: Cohen's d and 95% CI
TECS distribution: real vs Null-A overlay

tasks.name: Phase 4: TECS Ablation Study (4 Axes)
tasks.description: Test TECS robustness across 4 ablation axes: (1) Top-k cutoff: k in {5, 10, 20, 50}; (2) Weighting: BM25 vs uniform vs TF-IDF; (3) Loss: cross-entropy on object token vs full-sequence vs margin; (4) Gradient scope: layer l* only vs [l*-2, l*+2] aggregated. Compute Cohen's d for each setting. Robustness criterion: d variation < 20%.

pilot.pass_criteria: All 4 ablation axes computed AND TECS d variation < 30% (relaxed for pilot) AND no NaN/Inf values

visualizations.description: Ablation robustness: TECS Cohen's d across settings
Ablation sensitivity: Cohen's d per ablation setting

tasks.name: Phase 5: Rank-One Decomposition Validation
tasks.description: Test H2: compute decomposed TECS = cos(C^{-1}k*, k_i) * cos(v* - Wk*, d_v_i) for each fact. Compare with full TECS via Spearman rho. Compare empirical null variance with theoretical 1/d_k. Determine which component (key vs value alignment) carries more signal. CPU-only computation using pre-saved tensors.

pilot.pass_criteria: Spearman rho between decomposed and full TECS is computable AND key/value components have finite values AND empirical null variance within 10x of theoretical prediction

visualizations.description: Full TECS vs decomposed product, colored by key/value dominance
Key alignment vs value alignment component magnitudes

tasks.name: Positive Path: Dose-Response Analysis (H3)
tasks.description: Conditional on d > 0.2 from Phase 3. Compute partial Spearman correlation between TECS and ROME editing efficacy, controlling for pre-edit perplexity and relation type. Logistic regression P(edit_success) ~ TECS + covariates with LOOCV AUROC. Compare with Causal Tracing localization score as predictor. Threshold: partial rho > 0.2, delta_AUROC > 0.05.

pilot.pass_criteria: Partial Spearman correlation computable AND LOOCV AUROC computable AND no convergence failures in logistic regression

visualizations.description: TECS vs editing efficacy with regression line and CI band
Predictive comparison: TECS vs Causal Tracing vs covariates-only AUROC

tasks.name: Positive Path: Layer-Sweep Profile (H4, H9)
tasks.description: Conditional on d > 0.2. Compute TECS at all 48 GPT-2-XL layers for 50 representative facts. Compare layer profile with Causal Tracing indirect effect profile (Spearman rho on 48-layer vector). Test H4: TECS at l* > TECS at l*+-5 with Cohen's d > 0.3. Test H9: layer profile correlation with CT profile.

pilot.pass_criteria: TECS computable at all 48 layers AND no OOM AND layer profile shows non-uniform pattern (max/min ratio > 1.5)

visualizations.description: TECS layer profile across all 48 layers with Causal Tracing overlay

tasks.name: Positive Path: Spectral TECS Analysis (H5)
tasks.description: Conditional on d > 0.2. SVD of W at layer l*. Project delta_W_E and g_M onto 4 spectral bands: top-10, 10-50, 50-200, 200+ singular values. Compute band-wise TECS and Cohen's d. Test H5: mid-range (10-200) shows highest alignment. Use pre-computed SVD to avoid redundant computation.

pilot.pass_criteria: SVD of W at l* computable AND band-wise projections have non-zero norm AND spectral TECS values are finite

visualizations.description: Spectral TECS: alignment per singular value band

tasks.name: Negative Path: Subspace Geometry & Principal Angles (H7)
tasks.description: Conditional on d <= 0.2. Construct editing subspace S_E = span(delta_W_1,...,delta_W_N) and attribution subspace S_A = span(g_1,...,g_N) using top-r principal components (r by elbow criterion). Compute principal angles via scipy.linalg.subspace_angles. Compare theta_min against 100 random subspace samples. Test H7: structured (theta_min < random at p<0.01) vs random misalignment.

pilot.pass_criteria: Principal angles computable AND random baseline generated AND theta_min value is finite AND elbow criterion produces r in [5, 50]

visualizations.description: Principal angle distribution: S_E vs S_A compared to random subspace baseline

tasks.name: Negative Path: Whitening Decomposition (H6)
tasks.description: Conditional on d <= 0.2. Compute TECS_unwhitened using raw k* (without C^{-1}) in the editing direction construction. Compare TECS_whitened vs TECS_unwhitened via Cohen's d. Test H6: if unwhitened >> whitened (d > 0.3), ROME's covariance-inverse rotation explains the geometric gap. CPU computation on pre-saved tensors plus one GPU pass to extract C (key covariance matrix).

pilot.pass_criteria: C matrix extractable from EasyEdit internals AND TECS_unwhitened computable AND both distributions have finite mean and std

tasks.name: Negative Path: MEMIT Comparison
tasks.description: Conditional on d <= 0.2. Apply MEMIT (multi-layer editing) via EasyEdit to the same fact set. Measure alignment between MEMIT's distributed update vectors and TDA gradients across edited layers. Compare with ROME's single-layer alignment pattern. Tests whether distributed editing yields different geometric alignment.

pilot.pass_criteria: MEMIT editing succeeds on pilot set AND multi-layer alignment values computable AND efficacy > 70%

visualizations.description: ROME vs MEMIT alignment comparison across layers

tasks.name: Secondary: Consolidation Correlation (H8)
tasks.description: Test CLS theory prediction: facts with higher training frequency (BM25 retrieval count as proxy) show higher |TECS|. Spearman rho(|TECS|, BM25_count) controlling for pre-edit perplexity. Low priority, runs on pre-computed data.

pilot.pass_criteria: Correlation computable AND BM25 counts available for all facts

visualizations.description: BM25 retrieval count vs |TECS| with regression line
